what_it_does: Defines the Imagery canvas for ConceptV Studio, with tabbed lanes for a system image library, project-scoped imagery, 360-degree panorama hubs, and an AI Studio entry point. It manages uploads, seeded starter assets, project creation, panorama hotspot editing, image card actions, edit modal state, and status display for the OpenAI-backed Vi integration through browser globals.
observations: Uses React hook aliases from the global React object rather than module imports.
Registers the canvas globally as window.Imagery.
All image persistence operations go through window.cvImageStore.
OpenAI readiness is displayed through window.cvOpenAI configuration state, while AI Studio behavior is delegated to an external AIStudio component.
System and project lanes support drag-and-drop image upload through hidden file inputs.
The system lane includes a hardcoded starter set of image asset paths and tags.
Project images can be promoted into the system library.
360-degree hubs support project selection, panorama upload, active panorama selection, renaming, deletion, editable Pano360 hotspots, and a generated URL preview.
Several UI elements depend on globals such as CanvasHeader, CvIcon, ImageEditor, Pano360, ViShape, dsaToast, and shape components defined in this file.
standouts: Combines four imagery scopes in one tabbed canvas while keeping scope-specific lane components separate.
Uses the same store API shape across system, project, and hub imagery operations.
Includes panorama-specific editing concepts such as hotspots, longitude/latitude display, and equirectangular metadata.
conventions: Functional React components with local state and browser-global services.
File-local helper components for repeated cards, empty states, and small SVG shapes.
CSS class naming centers on cv-im-* for the imagery subsystem and dsa-* for shared controls.
Inline prompt dialogs are used for project creation, panorama naming, and hotspot labels.
Toast feedback is emitted through optional window.dsaToast calls.
Hidden file inputs are triggered by styled buttons and drop zones.
concerns: ProjectsLane renders project tag buttons without filter state or click handlers beyond the always-active All button.
Several global dependencies are used without local existence checks, including window.cvImageStore and window.cvOpenAI.
The subscription effects rely on the subscribe functions returning a valid cleanup function.
Upload handlers show success toasts based on the input file count even when individual imports fail.
The image card receives a scope prop but does not use it.
Project and hub selected project ids are initialized from the current project list and are not explicitly reconciled if the available projects change.
notes: This file is the UI coordinator for ConceptV imagery scopes, store-backed asset operations, and 360-degree hub editing.
questions: What contract do window.cvImageStore.subscribe and window.cvOpenAI.subscribe provide for cleanup?
Should project tag buttons filter imagery the way system tags do?
Is AIStudio guaranteed to be globally available before this canvas renders?
Should empty project and system lanes show EmptyStateImagery when lists are empty?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose largely matches the implementation: this file presents four imagery lanes and routes persistence through window.cvImageStore, while AI generation itself is referenced through AIStudio and OpenAI readiness rather than implemented here.
intention_signals: The file appears intended to make imagery a central, reusable studio subsystem where brand assets, project assets, panoramic hubs, and AI-generated material can be managed from one canvas.
novelty: It treats standard imagery, project assets, AI output, and editable 360-degree panoramas as neighboring lanes in the same management surface.
contribution: Provides a fused system with a store-backed imagery workspace, scoped upload flows, seedable brand references, project image promotion, panorama hotspot editing, image card actions, and shared visual language for asset management.
summary_for_embedding: A React canvas file defining ConceptV Studio imagery management across system library, project assets, 360-degree hubs, and AI Studio navigation using global store and OpenAI services.
intention_for_embedding: This was for centralizing brand, project, panorama, and AI-assisted imagery workflows into one tabbed production interface.

symbols.does: Renders the top-level Imagery canvas and switches between imagery lanes.
symbols.description: This component owns the active tab, edit modal state, and OpenAI readiness display. It subscribes to image store and OpenAI setting changes, renders the canvas header, tab bar, selected lane, and ImageEditor modal when an image is being edited.

symbols.does: Stores the current image editing target.
symbols.description: This helper captures the asset scope, project id, and image object into local editing state. That state controls whether ImageEditor is rendered and tells saveEdit where to persist the edited source.

symbols.does: Clears the current image editing state.
symbols.description: This helper closes the ImageEditor modal by setting editing to null. It does not perform persistence or other cleanup.

symbols.does: Persists an edited image source and closes the editor.
symbols.description: This function updates the current editing target in window.cvImageStore with a replacement src value. It then clears the modal state and emits an Image saved toast when available.

symbols.does: Renders the system-wide image library lane.
symbols.description: This component lists system images, filters them by predefined system tags, handles drag-and-drop and file input uploads, and exposes starter seeding. It renders the system toolbar, drop zone, and image grid with edit and remove actions.

symbols.does: Imports selected files into the system library.
symbols.description: This SystemLane-local async function iterates over provided image files and calls window.cvImageStore.addFromFile with system scope and a default Brand hero tag. It catches per-file import errors, displays failure toasts, and then reports the number of files submitted.

symbols.does: Adds a predefined starter set of brand reference images to the system library.
symbols.description: This SystemLane-local async function defines six hardcoded asset paths with names, tags, and starter source metadata. It imports each through window.cvImageStore.addFromSrc and reports the seeded count through a toast.

This component shows a project rail, selected project workspace, project image upload controls, a project drop zone, and an image grid. It also allows creating a project and promoting a project image into the system library.

symbols.does: Imports selected files into the active project.
symbols.description: This ProjectsLane-local async function adds each provided file to the projects scope for the selected project id. It reports individual import failures and then emits a toast naming the active project.

symbols.does: Creates a new imagery project from prompt input.
symbols.description: This ProjectsLane-local async function asks for a project name and optional client/developer label through browser prompts. It creates the project through window.cvImageStore.addProject and selects the newly created project id.

This component shows project selection for hub panoramas, a panorama thumbnail strip, upload control, active panorama detail card, editable Pano360 viewer, hotspot list, and URL preview. It manages selected project, active panorama, and local force refresh state after hotspot edits.

symbols.does: Imports selected files as hub panoramas for the active project.
symbols.description: This HubsLane-local async function adds each provided file to the hubs scope for the selected project id, using the filename without extension as the panorama name. It catches import failures and reports the submitted pano count through a toast.

symbols.does: Adds a hotspot to the active panorama.
symbols.description: This function prompts for a hotspot label and appends a new hotspot object with generated id, longitude, latitude, and label to the active panorama. It persists the updated hotspot array through window.cvImageStore.update and forces a render refresh.

symbols.does: Removes a hotspot from the active panorama.
symbols.description: This function filters the active panorama hotspot list by id and persists the remaining hotspots through window.cvImageStore.update. It also forces a local render refresh after the store update.
This HubsLane-local function prompts with the current panorama name and updates the active hub image name when a value is provided. It persists the rename through window.cvImageStore.update.

symbols.does: Renders an image thumbnail card with metadata and actions.
symbols.description: This shared component displays the image source, name, tags, AI badge when source is ai, dimensions, and overlay buttons for edit, optional promote, and remove. The action behavior is supplied by parent lane components.

symbols.does: Renders a scoped empty-state message for imagery lanes.
symbols.description: This component selects text for system, project, or hub empty states and displays it with a Diamond shape. It provides fallback copy when the supplied scope is not recognized.

symbols.does: Renders a small octagon SVG icon.
symbols.description: This file-local visual helper draws an outlined octagon using the bronze accent variable. It is used as the project rail shape marker.

symbols.does: Renders a diamond SVG icon with crossing dashed lines.
symbols.description: This file-local visual helper draws an outlined diamond and internal dashed diagonals using the bronze accent variable. It is used in the imagery empty state.
